description: Fix four bugs found during browser extension audit: confirmation badge count desync, unbounded seenConfirmationIds growth, permission rollback race condition in PluginCard, and stale tab count in side panel from ignored tabs array in tab.stateChanged handler.

userStories.title: Derive confirmation badge count from Map size instead of separate counter
userStories.description: Replace the separate pendingConfirmationCount counter with pendingConfirmations.size to eliminate count/map desync when clearConfirmationBadge is called without a valid id.
acceptanceCriteria: pendingConfirmationCount variable is removed from confirmation-badge.ts
All reads of pendingConfirmationCount use pendingConfirmations.size instead
clearConfirmationBadge(undefined) does not change the badge count or Map contents
clearAllConfirmationBadges still clears both the Map and the badge
Existing unit tests pass and cover the undefined-id edge case

userStories.title: Clean up seenConfirmationIds when confirmations are responded to
userStories.description: Remove confirmation IDs from the seenConfirmationIds deduplication Set when the corresponding confirmation is responded to, preventing unbounded growth.
acceptanceCriteria: seenConfirmationIds entries are removed when setPendingConfirmations removes the corresponding confirmation
Duplicate confirmations are still prevented while a confirmation is pending
seenConfirmationIds is cleared on disconnect (when setPendingConfirmations is reset to empty)
userStories.notes:
Files:
- platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/hooks/useServerNotifications.ts (add cleanup)
- platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/App.tsx (clear seenIds on disconnect)

Context:
`seenConfirmationIds` is a `useRef<Set<string>>` at line 29 of useServerNotifications.ts. It accumulates every confirmation ID to prevent duplicates (line 48-49):
```ts
if (seenConfirmationIds.current.has(params.id)) return;
seenConfirmationIds.current.add(params.id);
```
But IDs are never removed. The side panel can stay open for hours/days with hundreds of tool approvals.

Changes:
The cleanest approach is to expose a cleanup function from the hook that the caller uses when responding to confirmations. In `useServerNotifications`, return an additional `clearSeenId` function:
```ts
const clearSeenId = (id: string): void => {
  seenConfirmationIds.current.delete(id);
};
```
In App.tsx, `handleConfirmationRespond` (line 382) already calls `setPendingConfirmations(prev => prev.filter(c => c.id !== id))`. After that line, also call `clearSeenId(id)` to clean up the dedup set.

For the disconnect case, App.tsx line 341 already does `setPendingConfirmations([])`. Add a call to clear the entire set at that point. The hook should expose a `clearAllSeenIds` function that does `seenConfirmationIds.current.clear()`.

Constraints:
- Do NOT change the deduplication behavior â€” confirmations must still be deduped while pending
- The handleNotification function signature and return type from useServerNotifications must remain compatible with existing callers

userStories.title: Fix permission rollback race in PluginCard by storing per-operation rollback state
userStories.description: Replace shared preToggleRef/prePluginPermRef refs with per-operation rollback tracking so concurrent permission changes don't lose each other's rollback state.
acceptanceCriteria: When two tool permission changes are in-flight and the first fails, the first change is correctly rolled back without affecting the second
When two plugin permission changes are in-flight and the first fails, the first change is correctly rolled back
preToggleRef is replaced with a per-operation mechanism (e.g., Map keyed by version or operation id)
prePluginPermRef is replaced with a per-operation mechanism

userStories.title: Update tab.stateChanged handler to include tabs array from notification
userStories.description: Extend the tab.stateChanged handler in useServerNotifications to also update the plugin's tabs array from the notification params, so the side panel shows correct tab counts without waiting for getFullState.
acceptanceCriteria: When tab.stateChanged arrives with params.tabs, the plugin's tabs field is updated in addition to tabState
Tab count tooltip in PluginCard reflects the correct count immediately after tab open/close events
When params.tabs is absent or not an array, only tabState is updated (backwards compatible)
